The '''5YQ-series protocol droid''' was a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] series produced by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] as a short-lived extension of the company's successful [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series]].

==Characteristics==
The 5YQ protocol droid was similar in appearance to the 3PO-series in many respects, particularly in its 1.7-meter [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] frame. The use of shared components led to a lawsuit filed against Cybot Galactica, forcing the company to retire the line. Only a handful of 5YQ units remained in the market after this.<ref name="NEGD" />

==History==
These pewter-colored [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were designed to compete with [[Serv-O-Droid, Inc.]]'s [[Orbot]], and sold at low prices in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="NEGD" />

One of the most infamous 5YQ droids was [[I-5YQ]], nicknamed "I-Five" by his master, [[Lorn Pavan]]. Pavan heavily modified and upgraded his droid, until I-Five bore little more than a cosmetic similarity to its factory template.<ref name="NEGD" />
